title: Ludwigia greatrexii
taxonomy: Ludwigia greatrexii is classified under the family Onagraceae and belongs to the order Myrtales. It is a species of flowering plant native to Southeast Asia. Ludwigia greatrexii is further classified into the genus Ludwigia, which consists of various aquatic plants. The classification of Ludwigia greatrexii also includes the kingdom Plantae and the clade Angiosperms. This classification helps in understanding the evolutionary relationships among different plant species.
uses: Ludwigia greatrexii is known for its potential medicinal uses, although more research is required to confirm its effectiveness. It is sometimes used in traditional medicine, particularly in Asia, to treat various health conditions. properties: Ludwigia greatrexii has been studied for its potential therapeutic properties, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. constituents: Ludwigia greatrexii contains iridoids, specifically Ludwiganol, Ludwiginin and Ludwiginin-3-acetate. Further research is needed to fully understand the composition and effects of Ludwigia greatrexii's active constituents. parts: Ludwigia greatrexii is a species of aquatic plant that has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in Asia. preparations: Ludwigia greatrexii is a plant species that is primarily used for ornamental purposes in aquariums. However, limited information is available on its herbal preparations.
